student_name: Eliane Portela Nogueira Lima de Brito
hub_subtitle: Business English for a doctor rebuilding her professional voice

lesson.menu_title: Harvard, Stanford and Beyond — Speaking Up and Holding Your Own in Executive Education
lesson.menu_desc: Passive voice for professional and academic contexts, plus networking and speaking-up language for executive-education programs -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- Harvard, Stanford and Beyond
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eliane Portela | Aula 7 -- Harvard, Stanford and Beyond

listenings.text: Hi Eliane, and welcome. I am David, and I direct admissions for our executive-education program. Let me explain how the program is run, so you know what to expect. Applicants are evaluated on their leadership record, not only on test scores, so your work as a physician and a founder is a real strength. The curriculum is taught by leading faculty from the medical school and the business school together. Most of the learning is done through the case method: real cases are discussed in small groups, and every voice is expected to be heard. You are placed in a cohort of about sixty peers from all over the world, and lifelong connections are formed there. At the end, a capstone project is presented to the whole cohort. Do not be shy. In this room, quiet excellence is not enough; your ideas have to be spoken.

Hi Eliane, it is Sofia. We were placed in the same cohort last year, and you asked me what the experience is really like, so here are a few honest thoughts. The immersion is intense. Every case is prepared the night before and then debated the next morning, and at first I was terrified to speak. What helped me was a simple habit: I decided that one comment had to be made by me in every single session. Within two weeks, my hand was raised without fear. The keynote speakers are extraordinary, but the real value is the network. Coffee breaks are where the deals are made, honestly. My advice is this: your accreditation and your title open the door, but your voice is what makes people remember you. You will hold your own beautifully. Call me anytime.
